title: Subnetting

headings.text: How to Subnet: Step by Step

overview: Dividing a larger IP network into smaller, more manageable segments is called subnetting / think of it like cutting a big cake into precise slices so each department or group gets exactly what they need.

study.overview: Dividing a larger IP network into smaller, more manageable segments is called subnetting / think of it like cutting a big cake into precise slices so each department or group gets exactly what they need.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: How to Subnet: Step by Step
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
